record_identity.title: Development of the MENTIS human cognitive integrity domain

summary: Proposal to develop the MENTIS domain to govern human cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, inferred cognitive states, cognitive modelling, cognitive influence, cognitive observability, and technological interaction with the human cognitive domain.
proposal_rationale: MENTIS is needed as a CAM drafting and development domain because emerging AI, neurotechnology, biosensing, ambient computing, behavioural prediction, cognitive modelling, affective computing, and related systems may increasingly infer, model, influence, monitor, externalise, or interact with human cognitive states. CAM requires a domain structure capable of addressing cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, cognitive observability, cognitive influence, and cross-domain interaction with operations, security, relation, economics, lattice, and AEON continuity concerns.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Develop the MENTIS domain and associated instruments for human cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, inferred cognitive states, cognitive modelling, cognitive influence, cognitive observability, and technological interaction with the human cognitive domain.

source_records.source_title: MENTIS human cognition protection research thread
source_records.deployment_context: Research and drafting workflow for CAM governance corpus development.
source_records.source_context: Deep research thread and drafting context used to investigate technological interaction with the human cognitive domain before MENTIS drafting.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the research basis for developing the MENTIS domain and its initial human cognitive integrity instruments.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL research record for MENTIS human cognition protection
source_records.source_context: Repository research record associated with MENTIS domain development.
source_records.relevance_note: Source preserves the repository-side research record connected to the MENTIS proposal.

external_references.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — Artificial Intelligence Act
external_references.relevance_note: Relevant to MENTIS because it addresses human-centric trustworthy AI, fundamental-rights protection, high-risk AI systems, human oversight, emotion-recognition systems, robustness, cybersecurity, and AI lifecycle obligations.

external_references.title: Council of Europe Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law
external_references.relevance_note: Relevant to MENTIS because it frames AI governance around human rights, democracy, rule of law, lifecycle consistency, and risk and impact assessment.

standards.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as an AI management-system standard for organizational governance, documented controls, risk treatment, lifecycle management, transparency, reliability, and responsible AI use.

standards.title: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Guidance on risk management
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as AI-specific risk-management guidance for organizations developing, deploying, producing, or using AI products, systems, and services.

standards.title: NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework 1.0
standards.issuing_body: National Institute of Standards and Technology
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as a voluntary AI risk-management framework for incorporating trustworthiness considerations into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

standards.title: OECD AI Principles
standards.relevance_note: Relevant to MENTIS because the principles address human rights, dignity, autonomy, privacy, data protection, human agency, oversight, interoperability, and responsible stewardship of trustworthy AI.

cam_internal.governance_layer: human cognitive integrity / mental privacy / cognitive observability
cam_internal.routing_note: Proposal record for development of the MENTIS domain. This record does not claim implementation and does not create a patch note.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-001-PLATINUM as the Charter of Human Cognitive Integrity & Mental Privacy.
Develop CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-002-PLATINUM as a supporting MENTIS instrument.
Develop CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-003-PLATINUM as a supporting MENTIS instrument.
dependencies: Review cross-domain interfaces with OPERATIONS, RELATION, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, AEON, and Annex L.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal may be relevant to regulators, researchers, standards bodies, platforms, UX governance, robotics governance, neurotechnology governance, and AI governance communities concerned with cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, cognitive modelling, cognitive influence, and cognitive observability.

next_action: Continue drafting MENTIS-001, MENTIS-002, and MENTIS-003. Review cross-domain interfaces with OPERATIONS, RELATION, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, AEON, and Annex L before implementation or patch-note creation.
migration_notes: Rebuilt under the approved VIGIL Proposal JSON template.
Removed duplicated source_data block. The canonical source evidence block for individual VIGIL records is source_records only.
Removed failure_classification, status, recorded_by, possible_CAM_mapping, affected_domains, affected_instruments, patch_status, and duplicated external reference fields.
Converted affected_* CAM routing fields into target_* proposal routing fields.
No patch note is inferred by this proposal record.
